title: Vendor Remote Maintenance Window and Dual-Control Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for 4.4.3.2.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 4.4.3.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.10: Vendor Remote Maintenance Window and Dual-Control Evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.10' running on pan:globalprotect or pan:sslvpn; cyberark:psm, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295)
spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-14d
| lookup vendor_access.csv user OUTPUT vendor, window
| eval hod=strftime(_time,"%H")
| eval off_window=case(window="business" AND (hod<7 OR hod>18),1, window="24x7",0, true(),0)
| join type=left user [ search index=cyberark sourcetype="cyberark:psm" earliest=-14d | stats count as psm_hits by user ]
| where vendor!="Internal" AND (off_window=1 OR psm_hits=0)
| stats count by vendor, user, off_window
description: Monitors vendor VPN and PSM sessions against contracted hours and approval records to evidence supplier risk management for OT maintenance.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cyberark, vpn and sourcetypes cyberark:psm, pan:globalprotect. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Populate vendor contract lookup; (2) force vendor IDs through PSM; (3) alert security + procurement on violations; (4) quarterly supplier review export. Indexes required: `index=cyberark`, `index=vpn`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cyberark:psm`, `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`.
visualization: Bar (by vendor), Table, Single value (weekly violations).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: PAM sessions from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:globalprotect` or `pan:sslvpn`; `cyberark:psm`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Populate vendor contract lookup; (2) force vendor IDs through PSM; (3) alert security + procurement on violations; (4) quarterly supplier review export.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-14d
| lookup vendor_access.csv user OUTPUT vendor, window
| eval hod=strftime(_time,"%H")
| eval off_window=case(window="business" AND (hod<7 OR hod>18),1, window="24x7",0, true(),0)
| join type=left user [ search index=cyberark sourcetype="cyberark:psm" earliest=-14d | stats count as psm_hits by user ]
| where vendor!="Internal" AND (off_window=1 OR psm_hits=0)
| stats count by vendor, user, off_window
```

Understanding this SPL

**Vendor Remote Maintenance Window and Dual-Control Evidence (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.4.3.2)** — Monitors vendor VPN and PSM sessions against contracted hours and approval records to evidence supplier risk management for OT maintenance.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:globalprotect` or `pan:sslvpn`; `cyberark:psm`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vpn; **sourcetype**: pan:globalprotect. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vpn, sourcetype="pan:globalprotect", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hod** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **off_window** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where vendor!="Internal" AND (off_window=1 OR psm_hits=0)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **vendor, user, off_window**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Vendor Remote Maintenance Window and Dual-Control Evidence (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.4.3.2)** — Monitors vendor VPN and PSM sessions against contracted hours and approval records to evidence supplier risk management for OT maintenance.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:globalprotect` or `pan:sslvpn`; `cyberark:psm`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: ], hod, off_window, psm_hits, user, vendor.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with CyberArk PVWA / session recording for the same user and time window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar (by vendor), Table, Single value (weekly violations).
grandmaExplanation: We watch vendor VPN and PSM sessions against contracted hours and approval records to evidence supplier risk management for OT maintenance. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Authentication data model (Authentication.Authentication).
